title: Spellcasting Focus
excerpt: A spellcasting focus is an allowed object that stands in for eligible material components when a spell is cast. It helps satisfy the spell's component requirement but does not override restrictions the spell text keeps in force.
markdown:
# Spellcasting Focus

A spellcasting focus is a permitted object that can stand in for a spell's eligible material components when that [[spell]] is cast. The focus does not become a new kind of magic rule by itself; it is one way the caster satisfies the material-component part of the casting requirement.

## What A Focus Does

If a spell's material component has no listed cost and is not consumed, an allowed focus can usually satisfy that requirement instead of the caster handling the listed item directly.

## What A Focus Does Not Do

A focus does not remove other casting requirements:
- It does not replace verbal or somatic components
- It does not satisfy costly materials the spell specifically requires
- It does not preserve a cast that already fails some other rule of timing, targeting, or availability

## Rules Surface

The full component rules live under [[spell_components_rules]]. This entry exists as the narrower bridge term for cases where rules text, class features, or item text specifically call out a spellcasting focus.
